title: Nemcia heterophylla
intro: Nemcia heterophylla, also known as the "Kakadu plum," is a small, deciduous tree native to the northern regions of Australia. The Kakadu plum has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its health benefits, which include treating fever, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's bioactive constituents include high levels of vitamin C, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids. The most common herbal preparations made with the Kakadu plum include extracts, powders, and capsules, which are often used to support immune function and overall health.
